channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Deploying a full, three-tier book review web app on Azure
item.description: I designed and deployed one of my most hands-on cloud engineering projects: deploying a secure, scalable book review application using a full three-tier architecture on Microsoft Azure. But the...

item.title: I Built an AI That Designs Charts From Scratch — No Templates, No D3, Just Words
item.description: Most "AI chart tools" are glorified template fillers. You pick a chart type, paste your CSV, and the AI picks colors. That's not intelligence — that's a dropdown with extra steps. I wanted...

item.title: Build an Automated Order Fulfilment Workflow in Shopify with These 3 APIs
item.description: (Fulfilment? Fulfillment?) Anyways, since I started with Shopify clients, I have observed many of them fulfill orders the same way: They log into the dashboard Check the order Manually copy the...

item.title: How I Slashed Gas Costs by 25% for a Solidity Merkle Mixer
item.description: Gas Surgery: Reducing Merkle Mixer Costs by 25% on Base Building a privacy mixer is a challenge of balance. You want strong anonymity, but you don't want the user to pay a fortune in gas for a...

item.title: Managing database schema changes in .NET From theory to FluentMigrator
item.description: If you’ve ever managed a database alongside an evolving application, you’ve likely faced a common issue: your code and your database don't always evolve at the same pace. Over time, the schema...

item.title: The Prow Upgrade Story
item.description: One of my first experiences with Kubernetes was with using Prow. Prow is a Kubernetes CI/CD system. In the Mesosphere/D2IQ days, I had to transition the CI jobs that we were running in Jenkins on...

item.title: Why Trenchless Pipeline Installation is the Future of Utility Projects
item.description: Cities keep growing. Roads, buildings, and public services need constant upgrades. But digging long trenches for pipelines creates problems. It blocks traffic, damages roads, and disturbs nearby...

item.title: GPU Flight — System Architecture
item.description: The previous post was about thread divergence at the SASS level. Before I move on to other optimization strategies, I think it makes sense to first review GPU Flight’s architecture. Understanding...

item.title: GPU Flight — System Architecture
item.description: The previous post was about thread divergence at the SASS level. Before I move on to other optimization strategies, I think it makes sense to first review GPU Flight’s architecture. Understanding...

item.title: Bragging About Killing Jobs Is a Bad Look. It's Also Bad Strategy.
item.description: An AI video startup posted something to the effect of 'we ended jobs' and seemed surprised when people got angry about it. The backlash was predictable. What's less predictable is why any company...

item.title: Google, Accel India accelerator chooses 5 startups and none are ‘AI wrappers’
item.description: Google and Accel say about 70% of AI startup pitches tied to India were "wrappers" as they reviewed more than 4,000 applications for their Atoms cohort.

item.title: ByteDance reportedly pauses global launch of its Seedance 2.0 video generator
item.description: The company is reportedly delaying the launch as its engineers and lawyers work to avert further legal issues.

item.title: Aether OS is a computer in a browser built for the AT Protocol
item.description: Aether OS puts a full-fledged desktop in your browser that ties directly into the AT Protocol. That means it connects to your Bluesky account and other public records. It offers a pretty full...

item.title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
item.description: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

item.title: Sotomayor’s Wabi Sabi is the funnest record of 2026
item.description: Shout out to subscriber N_Gorski for today's pick. They popped into the comments on last week's recommendation to ask what I thought of the new Sotomayor record. Well, I hadn't actually heard it...

item.title: Wiz investor unpacks Google’s $32B acquisition
item.description: Shardul Shah of Index Ventures walks us through Google's biggest acquisition ever.

item.title: AI Czar David Sacks wants Trump to ‘get out’ of Iran
item.description: David Sacks, the White House's AI and crypto czar, has warned that a continued war in Iran could be catastrophic. On the All In podcast, Sacks said that "we should try to find the off-ramp." He...

item.title: AI companies want to harvest improv actors’ skills to train AI on human emotion
item.description: If you've got strong creative instincts, the ability to authentically portray emotion, and are capable of staying true to a character's voice throughout a scene, there's a job listing calling for...

item.title: Samsung’s Galaxy Buds 4 Pro are the choice — if you have a Galaxy phone
item.description: Samsung promised "more than a design refresh" for its new top-end Galaxy Buds - it added upgraded woofers for improved, dual-amped hi-fi sound, a new blade design, clearer call performance, and...

item.title: The fast rise and epic fall of Clubhouse
item.description: In 2020 and 2021, the social media world seemed to be on the verge of complete change. A new app called TikTok was ascendant, bringing a whole new kind of vertical video to phones everywhere. And...

item.title: Live-service games are a mess
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the slow-motion disaster of live-service games, follow Andrew Webster. The Stepback...

item.title: The $100,000 fee for H-1Bs is causing all sorts of problems
item.description: Last fall, President Donald Trump's executive order raising the fee for H-1B visas to $100,000 - like many of his immigration policies - led to near-immediate chaos. Thousands of workers who had...

item.title: Trump administration is allegedly collecting $10 billion on the TikTok deal
item.description: In September, Donald Trump claimed that "the United States is getting a tremendous fee" for brokering the TikTok deal. Now sources tell the Wall Street Journal and the New York Times that fee is...

item.title: US Army announces contract with Anduril worth up to $20B
item.description: The Army described this as a single enterprise contract consolidating more than 120 separate "procurement actions."

item.title: Meta is reportedly laying off up to 20 percent of its staff
item.description: According to Reuters, Meta is looking to offset spending on AI and data centers with a massive round of layoffs. Sources familiar with the matter say the company could lay off as much as 20...

item.title: Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company
item.description: These layoffs could help Facebook's parent company offset its aggressive spending on AI infrastructure, as well as AI-related acquisitions and hiring.

item.title: OpenClaw AI Agent Flaws Could Enable Prompt Injection and Data Exfiltration
item.description: China's National Computer Network Emergency Response Technical Team (CNCERT) has issued a warning about the security stemming from the use of OpenClaw (formerly Clawdbot and Moltbot), an...

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.description: This is a current list of where and when I am scheduled to speak: I’m giving the Ross Anderson Lecture at the University of Cambridge’s Churchill College at 5:30 PM GMT on Thursday, March 19,...

item.title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
item.description: Learn how to use Spotify, Canva, Figma, Expedia, and other apps directly in ChatGPT.

item.title: GlassWorm Supply-Chain Attack Abuses 72 Open VSX Extensions to Target Developers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new iteration of the GlassWorm campaign that they say represents a "significant escalation" in how it propagates through the Open VSX registry. "Instead of...
